findings.title: No code for the Section 3.3 / Figure 7 iterated SD-KDE experiment
findings.quote:
As shown in Figure 7, repeated
application of SD-KDE yields a closer alignment between the estimated and true probability densities
and a corresponding reduction in KL divergence and mean integrated squared error (MISE) with each
iteration.
findings.claim: Section 3.3 and Figure 7 report an iterated/multi-step SD-KDE procedure (recompute score, re-apply SD-KDE, KL and MISE over 1-4 iterations, 200 MC replicates); a grep of both code trees finds zero iteration loops in any .py file and no script that produces the per-iteration KL/MISE curves.
findings.concern: A reported experiment and its figure have no code that computes the underlying values in either released tree, so Figure 7 cannot be reproduced.
findings.resolution: Authors: please add the iterated-SD-KDE script (score recomputation loop + KL/MISE-vs-iteration aggregation over the 200 replicates) used for Figure 7.
findings.reason: Repo-wide grep of both code trees (Elliotepsteino__SD-KDE and SD-KDE__neurips_supplemental) finds zero matches for iteration-loop patterns (for.*iter, n_iter, iteration, recompute, successive) in any .py file; paper quote confirmed at paper_text.txt lines 402-405; Figure 7 has no reproducible code in either tree.

findings.title: No code ranks MNIST images by SD-KDE density (Section 3.4 / Figure 16)
findings.quote:
Using this score, we apply SD-KDE in latent space to assess the realism of generated images. We ranked generated
images from highest to lowest estimated probability density, visualized in Figure 16.
findings.claim: Section 3.4 / Figure 16 report applying SD-KDE in latent space to score and rank generated MNIST images by estimated density; the released MNIST code (train_mnist.py, superminddpm.py) only trains/samples a DDPM, and a grep of both trees finds no argsort/rank/latent-density/SD-KDE-on-MNIST code that produces the ranking.
findings.concern: The MNIST experiment's reported result (density-ordered image grid) has no code computing the per-image SD-KDE density or the ranking in either tree, so Figure 16 / Section 3.4 is not reproducible.
findings.resolution: Authors: please add the script that computes the latent-space SD-KDE density per generated MNIST image and produces the ranked grid in Figure 16.
findings.reason: Repo-wide grep of both code trees finds zero matches for argsort, rank, latent_space, density-ranking, or highest-to-lowest patterns in any .py file; paper quote confirmed at paper_text.txt lines 446-448; no latent-space SD-KDE density or ranking script exists in either released tree.

findings.title: 2D Fig-6 notebook hardcodes /scratch model paths; shipped checkpoints sit elsewhere
findings.claim: The Figure-6 spiral comparison loads the diffusion-score checkpoint from an absolute path '/scratch/Score_KDE/minDiffusion/notebooks/best_model_spirals.pth', but the supplement ships that checkpoint at the relative path minDiffusion/notebooks/best_model_spirals.pth; KDEComparison.__init__ constructs DiffusionScoreEstimator (which torch.load's the path) before computing any MISE, so the whole spiral panel crashes with FileNotFoundError when run as shipped.
findings.concern: Figure 6 (the headline 2D diffusion-score result) cannot be reproduced from the released artefact without editing the hardcoded path, even though the needed checkpoint is included.
findings.resolution: Authors: derive the checkpoint path from BASE_DIR / a relative path so the included best_model_*.pth files are found out of the box (24 such /scratch|/pscratch paths exist across the diffusion/UCI code).
findings.reason: Quote confirmed at 2d.py lines 1133-1137 (__main__ block) and also at module-level lines 907-911; the actual checkpoints exist at code/SD-KDE__neurips_supplemental/minDiffusion/notebooks/best_model_*.pth (relative), while the code hardcodes /scratch/Score_KDE/... absolute paths; grep finds 24 such hardcoded paths across the two trees, matching the auditor's count.

findings.title: Figure 3 caption/text say n=100, but the code produces it with n=200
findings.claim: The MISE-difference histograms (Figure 3) are computed with n_example=200 (shrinkage_kde_gaussian.py:189, used at lines 246-259), and my reproduction at n=200 matches the figure's reported statistics (M1 mean 0.015/std 0.002, M2 0.015/0.001, M3 0.009/std~0.005, ~96% of seeds better); at n=100 the statistics differ (M1 mean 0.019, M3 ~84%). The paper's Figure-3 caption and body instead state 'for n = 100 samples' and 'better in 95% of samples'.
findings.concern: The reported sample size for Figure 3 (n=100) does not match the n=200 that the code actually used to produce the matching numbers; the qualitative claim ('SD-KDE consistently beats Silverman') holds at both n, so impact is low.
findings.resolution: Authors: correct the Figure-3 caption/text to n=200 (or regenerate the figure at n=100), and reconcile the '95%' / 'all 100 samples' wording with the 50 seeds the code uses (n_seeds=50).
findings.paper_ref: Figure 3 caption and Section 3.1 'SD-KDE consistently beats Silverman baseline'
findings.reason: Quote confirmed at shrinkage_kde_gaussian.py lines 189-190; n_example=200 used in the Fig-3 MISE loop at lines 246-259; running check_gaussian_1d.py reproduces the paper statistics (M3 frac=0.960, means ~0.015/0.014/0.010) at n=200 but not at n=100 (M3 frac=0.840); paper_text.txt lines 252/315/1049 all state n=100.
